perturbation.original_text: Each party may disclose Conﬁdential Information only on a need-to-know basis to its own employees and to its consultants under appropriate written burden of conﬁdentiality. Each party shall take appropriate action with its employees and advisors to satisfy its obligations under this Agreement. Each party shall be responsible to the other for any violation of this Agreement by its own employees or consultants.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party may disclose Conﬁdential Information to its employees and consultants. Each party shall take action with its employees and advisors to satisfy its obligations under this Agreement. Each party shall be responsible to the other for any violation of this Agreement by its own managers.
perturbation.explanation: The changes remove the 'need-to-know basis', the 'appropriate written burden of confidentiality' for consultants, 'appropriate' for action, and limits violation responsiblity to only 'managers' creating an in-text contradiction by omitting the need of confidentiality when disclosing information to certain parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party may disclose Conﬁdential Information to its employees and consultants. Each party shall take action with its employees and advisors to satisfy its obligations under this Agreement. Each party shall be responsible to the other for any violation of this Agreement by its own managers.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes key requirements related to confidentiality and responsibility compared to the original text. Specifically, it eliminates the 'need-to-know basis' for disclosing confidential information, the 'appropriate written burden of confidentiality' for consultants, replaces 'appropriate action' with only 'action', and limits violation responsibility to only 'managers'. These omissions directly contradict the implied obligation to protect confidential information and ensure responsibility for all employees and consultants who violate the agreement, thus creating an in-text contradiction within the document by omitting the need for confidentiality when disclosing information to certain parties. Since the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text are the same, we can see the change itself is what causes the contradiction, as described in the explanation.
